summary: Drew DeVault gives his April 2020 monthly status update while reflecting briefly on the isolation of lockdown life. He reports progress on his Wayland book (input chapter finished), continued improvements to wlroots and Sway, and significant SourceHut work including hub.sr.ht and an experimental GraphQL API to replace the REST design. He also teases his 'secret project' — what would become the Hare programming language — with a code snippet showing manual memory management and C-like syntax.
key_points: The Wayland book's input chapter is complete, with XDG positioner, drag-and-drop, and protocol extensions remaining
SourceHut is experimenting with GraphQL APIs to replace the REST design, citing better service interoperability as a core need
hub.sr.ht is progressing well and nearing an early release
The 'secret project' (later revealed as Hare) is teased with a code sample showing pointers, manual memory management, and C interop
money_quotes: I'm starting to lose track of the days stuck inside.
I have felt that the current REST API design was less than ideal, and the best time to change it would be during the alpha.
The GraphQL design, while it has its limitations, is a lot better than the REST design and should make it a lot easier for services to interop with each other - which is a core design need for sr.ht.
Remember to call your friends and family and let them know how you're doing.
The DRM (not the bad kind) implementation continues to see improvements, expanding to more and more use-cases with even better performance.
key_insight: DeVault's April 2020 update captures a prolific period of parallel progress across Wayland, SourceHut's API redesign, and the earliest public teases of what would become the Hare programming language.
